---
title: "AlgaeBot: A Hybrid GraphRAG System for Domain-Specific Question Answering in Algae Research"
author: "Filip Nový"
date: today
institute: "University of Southern Denmark"
supervisor: "Tariq Youssef"

format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: report
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    lof: true          # list of figures
    lot: true          # list of tables
    geometry: margin=2.5cm
    fontsize: 12pt
    linestretch: 1.5
    indent: true
    keep-tex: false
    cite-method: biblatex
    biblio-style: apa

bibliography: references.bib   # your .bib file goes here
lang: en
---

\newpage

# Abstract {.unnumbered}

```{=html}
<!--
~250 words. Write last. Cover: motivation (why algae, why RAG), 
what you built, how you evaluated it, and key findings.
-->
```

*\[Write last. \~250 words. Summarise: the problem, your approach (hybrid GraphRAG), the evaluation framework (HOPE + RAGAS), and your main findings.\]*

\newpage

# Introduction

## Background and Motivation

```{=html}
<!--
Set the scene: the algae research domain, the volume of literature in the 
*Algae* journal corpus (~879 PDFs), and why a domain-specific chatbot is valuable.
Why is generic search or a general-purpose LLM insufficient?
-->
```

## Research Questions

```{=html}
<!--
State 2–3 focused RQs. Something like:
RQ1: Can a hybrid GraphRAG architecture improve context recall over vector-only RAG 
     for domain-specific algae queries?
RQ2: Which chunking and embedding strategy yields the strongest retrieval performance 
     as measured by the HOPE framework?
RQ3: How does structured knowledge extracted via LLM-guided entity extraction 
     complement unstructured vector retrieval?
-->
```

## Thesis Structure

```{=html}
<!--
One short paragraph walking the reader through the chapters.
-->
```

\newpage

# Related Work

## Retrieval-Augmented Generation

```{=html}
<!--
Core RAG paper (Lewis et al. 2020). Dense passage retrieval (Karpukhin et al.).
RAG survey (Gao et al.). Explain the two-step retrieve-then-generate paradigm
and its limitations (e.g. context recall ceiling without structured knowledge).
-->
```

## Chunking Strategies

```{=html}
<!--
Cover: fixed-size recursive splitting, semantic chunking (percentile threshold),
and the Recursive Semantic Chunking approach (Latif et al.) that you implemented.
Note the role of overlap, chunk size, and the effect on retrieval space inflation.
-->
```

## Embedding Models and Re-ranking

```{=html}
<!--
Sentence-BERT / bi-encoder paradigm (Reimers & Gurevych).
BGE model family. Cross-encoder re-ranking.
MultiQuery retrieval as a query expansion strategy.
-->
```

## Knowledge Graphs in NLP

```{=html}
<!--
Hogan et al. on knowledge graphs broadly.
GraphRAG approaches — how graph traversal complements vector search.
Ontology-grounded relation design (RO, BFO, ENVO, ChEBI, GO).
-->
```

## Domain Context: Algae Research Literature

```{=html}
<!--
Brief note on the *Algae* journal, the corpus, and why the domain 
is challenging (multilingual content, OCR variability, taxonomic specificity).
-->
```

\newpage

# Data

## Corpus Description

```{=html}
<!--
~879 PDFs from the Korean journal *Algae*.
Publication span, language mix (English + Korean), approximate token volume.
Why newest-to-oldest processing order was chosen (OCR cost for pre-2007 scans).
-->
```

## PDF Extraction Pipeline

```{=html}
<!--
UnstructuredPDFLoader with fast-first / OCR-fallback strategy.
Poppler + Tesseract for scanned documents.
Preprocessing: removal of references, acknowledgements, and figure captions.
LLM-based metadata extraction (title, authors, year, volume) via Gemma 3:1b.
LLM-generated retrieval summaries (Gemma 3:1b prompt design and iteration).
-->
```

## Corpus Statistics

```{=html}
<!--
Table: total documents, documents requiring OCR, average pages,
token distribution before/after preprocessing.
-->
```

\newpage

# Methodology

## System Architecture Overview

```{=html}
<!--
High-level diagram: PDF corpus → extraction → chunking → embedding → ChromaDB
                                                         ↘ KG extraction → Neo4j
Query → MultiQuery reformulation → vector retrieval → re-ranking → (KG lookup) → generation
-->
```

## Chunking

### Recursive Text Splitting

```{=html}
<!--
LangChain RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter. Chunk size 1000, overlap 0.
Why overlap = 0 was chosen (redundancy cost in retrieval space).
-->
```

### Semantic Chunking

```{=html}
<!--
Percentile-95 threshold. Results before and after preprocessing.
Why it underperformed on this corpus (inter-chunk dependencies, short tail chunks).
-->
```

### Recursive Semantic Chunking (RSC)

```{=html}
<!--
Latif et al. approach: sentence-boundary splitting within a max-token budget.
Implementation and observed chunk size distribution.
-->
```

## Evaluation Framework: HOPE

```{=html}
<!--
Your custom framework. Define the three metrics:
ζ_con (coherence), ζ_sem (semantic density), ζ_inf (informativeness via retrieval).
Justify why intrinsic + retrieval-based evaluation is necessary together.
Present results table: recursive_1000 HOPE=0.8250 as best performer.
-->
```

## Embedding

```{=html}
<!--
BGE model family comparison: bge-base-en-v1.5 vs bge-m3.
Why ζ_con and ζ_sem are embedding-independent (chunk-intrinsic).
bge-m3 multilingual test results (Korean queries). Final choice: bge-base-en-v1.5.
ChromaDB collection naming (note the recursive_100 / recursive_1000 quirk if relevant).
-->
```

## Retrieval

### Dense Vector Retrieval

```{=html}
<!--
ChromaDB similarity search. Top-k selection.
-->
```

### MultiQuery Expansion

```{=html}
<!--
Gemma 3:1b via Ollama generating multiple reformulations of each query.
Why this addresses vocabulary mismatch and boosts recall.
-->
```

### Cross-Encoder Re-ranking

```{=html}
<!--
BGE cross-encoder. How it re-scores retrieved candidates before passing to generation.
Effect on latency (measured?) and answer quality.
-->
```

## Prompt Engineering

```{=html}
<!--
Systematic prompt iteration documented in your log.
Role: marine biologist persona. Task decomposition (assess → synthesise → flag conflicts).
Key additions: "do not cite unused sources", scope rejection for out-of-domain queries,
persona self-identification as "Algaebot".
Reference the taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques paper you read.
-->
```

## Generation

```{=html}
<!--
GPT-5-nano via OpenAI API as primary generator.
Local toggle: Gemma 3:1b / Qwen3-4B via Ollama (CPU limitations noted).
Config.py toggle design (USE_LOCAL_GENERATION flag).
-->
```

## Knowledge Graph Construction

### Schema Design

```{=html}
<!--
7 relationship types grounded in real ontologies.
RO, BFO, ENVO, ChEBI, GO, SO — which relation types came from which ontology.
Deliberate decision not to invent relations without ontological grounding.
-->
```

### Entity and Relation Extraction

```{=html}
<!--
DeepSeek API with Pydantic + instructor for structured output.
Processing order: newest-to-oldest (same as extraction pipeline, avoids OCR noise).
Prompt design for extraction. Example extracted triple.
-->
```

### Graph Storage and Querying

```{=html}
<!--
Neo4j. Node and edge types. Cypher query patterns used during retrieval.
-->
```

## Hybrid Retrieval (GraphRAG)

```{=html}
<!--
How vector results and graph traversal are combined.
Does graph lookup happen in parallel or as a fallback?
How conflicts/redundancy between the two sources are handled at the prompt level.
-->
```

\newpage

# Evaluation

## Baseline RAG Results (RAGAS)

```{=html}
<!--
faithfulness: 0.852, answer_relevancy: NaN (explain why), 
context_precision: 0.7277, context_recall: 0.5745.
What NaN answer_relevancy means methodologically — address it explicitly.
Interpretation: faithful generation but retrieval gaps motivate the KG component.
-->
```

## GraphRAG vs. Baseline Comparison

```{=html}
<!--
Same RAGAS metrics on the hybrid system.
Delta table. Which metrics improved and why.
If context_recall improved: attribute to graph traversal filling retrieval gaps.
-->
```

## Ablation Analysis

```{=html}
<!--
Chunking strategy × embedding model results (HOPE framework).
Re-ranking: with vs without (retrieval quality delta).
MultiQuery: with vs without (context recall delta).
-->
```

## Qualitative Analysis

```{=html}
<!--
A small set of representative Q&A examples:
- A factual taxonomic query (Zostera marina)
- A comparison query (CO2 fixation by 5 species)
- A scope-rejection query (weather in Punta Arenas)
- A multilingual query (Czech / Korean)
Discuss where the system succeeds and where it still fails.
-->
```

## Limitations

```{=html}
<!--
context_recall ceiling without exhaustive graph coverage.
GPU dependency for local generation.
RAGAS answer_relevancy NaN — note as a measurement gap.
Corpus language bias (primarily English-language papers about Korean species).
-->
```

\newpage

# Discussion

```{=html}
<!--
Interpret results in relation to your RQs.
Why did recursive_1000 outperform semantic chunking in this domain?
What does the RAGAS recall gap tell us about pure vector RAG for specialised corpora?
What does the graph add — and what does it fail to add?
Implications for domain-specific RAG system design more broadly.
-->
```

\newpage

# Conclusion

```{=html}
<!--
~1 page. Restate contributions:
1. HOPE evaluation framework for chunking strategy selection
2. Systematic embedding and retrieval ablation
3. Ontology-grounded knowledge graph schema and extraction pipeline
4. Hybrid GraphRAG system (AlgaeBot) with Streamlit interface
State what worked, what didn't, and the most important lessons learned.
-->
```

## Future Work

```{=html}
<!--
GPU-accelerated local generation (UCloud / HPC).
Expanding graph coverage to improve context recall.
Agentic chunking (you flagged as promising but expensive).
Fine-tuning an embedding model on algae literature.
-->
```

\newpage

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::

\newpage

# Appendix {.unnumbered}

## Appendix A: HOPE Metric Definitions and Formulas {.unnumbered}

## Appendix B: Prompt Templates {.unnumbered}

```{=html}
<!--
Final versions of: extraction prompt, generation prompt, MultiQuery prompt.
-->
```

## Appendix C: Chunking Statistics {.unnumbered}

```{=html}
<!--
Chunk size distributions per strategy (histograms or tables).
-->
```

## Appendix D: System Configuration {.unnumbered}

```{=html}
<!--
config.py parameters used in final evaluation.
-->
```
